description: Apply quicksort to [3,1,2]. Expected output: [1,2,3]. This file composes the qs function (from quicksort.nela.json) with a concrete input list. The graph is closed (no free ports) — it reduces to a Nil/Cons normal form.

Nat.note: concrete natural number — tagged with value in node role

nodes.role: the quicksort function, imported from quicksort.nela.json via fix_qs.p

reduction_trace.description: Symbolic trace of the first reduction steps. Each step lists the active pair that fires.

steps.note: Fix unrolls: lam_self receives a Dup-managed copy of fix_qs as self

steps.state: lam_self receives self-reference via Fix; lam_lst becomes active
steps.note: After Fix><Lam: self wired to dup_s; result = lam_lst. App(lam_lst, cons3) forms next redex
Beta: lst variable (= match.p) gets cons3; match.a0 becomes new result
Cons rule: Era(nil_r); h=3 wired to lam_h var; t=Cons(1,Cons(2,Nil)) wired to lam_t var; lam_h(lam_t(...)) applied to h and t
Two beta reductions: h=3 enters dup_h; t=Cons(1,...) enters dup_t

steps.state: dup_h fires: h1=3 to fle; h2=3 to dup_h2 | dup_t fires: t1=Cons(1,Cons(2,Nil)) to fle; t2=same to fgt
steps.note: Dup><Cons rules propagate duplication structurally through t (the list)
Parallel: both filters run simultaneously by confluence theorem
Two recursive Fix unrollings: fully independent active pairs => parallel

steps.state: qs([]) => Nil (base case, 2 steps) | qs([1,2]) recurses (pivot=1, left=[], right=[2])
steps.note: Full trace omitted; normal form reached in O(n log n) parallel steps
No active pairs remain. Result is a linear chain of Cons/Nat/Nil agents.

linearity_status: CLOSED NET — no free ports in edge set excluding app_top.p (the result); valid for execution
